name: New Brunswick (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the field of the provincial arms
the lion of the arms and the heraldic waves below the galley

description: A horizontal flag with two bands: an upper yellow band twice the height of the lower band and bearing a red lion passant; the lower red band bears a yellow-and-blue lymphad (ancient single-masted galley) under sail on stylised waves.
history: Adopted on 24 February 1965, the design is a banner of the arms of the province of New Brunswick, granted by Queen Victoria in 1868. New Brunswick was among the first provinces to adopt a distinctive provincial flag distinct from the Canadian Red Ensign tradition, in advance of Canada's own change to the Maple Leaf flag in 1965.

features.symbolism: the colours and proportions of the provincial coat of arms (a gold field with a lion and waves below); the lower red band represents the waves charged onto a galley

features.role: lion passant guardant in the upper yellow band, taken from the arms of New Brunswick
features.symbolism: the historical link to the House of Brunswick (Hanover) and to England, as on the provincial coat of arms granted by Queen Victoria in 1868.

features.role: lymphad (ancient galley) with sails set on heraldic waves, in the lower red band
features.symbolism: the importance of shipbuilding and seafaring to New Brunswick's history; the galley and the wavy red and yellow base recall the lower portion of the provincial arms.
